---
title: "Why I Believe"
pagetitle: "Why I Believe — Quan (Kyle) Zhang"
---

I am a Christian. My faith is the foundation of how I understand my work and the
people my research is ultimately for. I regularly attend and help with a Christian
campus fellowship at the University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa, and I take part in
Reformed University Fellowship (RUF) activities.

## Kindred voices

A few scholars whose writing on faith and academic vocation I have found worth
reading — each link below is freely readable:

::: {.pub-list}
::: {.pub}
**Jay Bhattacharya** — health economist (Stanford) and Director of the U.S. National
Institutes of Health — on his conversion and seeing patients as image-bearers.
[Read the interview →](https://www.christianitytoday.com/2025/06/nih-director-jay-bhattacharya-trump-covid-health-medicine/)
:::

::: {.pub}
**Tyler J. VanderWeele** — Professor of Epidemiology, Harvard, and Director of the
Human Flourishing Program — author of *A Theology of Health: Wholeness and Human
Flourishing* (Univ. of Notre Dame Press, 2024).
[Free book excerpt →](https://undpress.nd.edu/blog/2024/10/09/an-excerpt-from-a-theology-of-health-by-tyler-j-vanderweele/) ·
[Author interview →](https://undpress.nd.edu/blog/2024/09/18/an-interview-with-tyler-j-vanderweele-author-of-a-theology-of-health/)
:::

::: {.pub}
**Glenn C. Loury** — Professor of Economics, Brown University — his essay
*"Hope in the Unseen: On Being a Christian and an Economist."*
[Read the essay →](https://1776unites.org/essays/hope-in-the-unseen-on-being-a-christian-and-an-economist-with-an-intro-by-dr-anthony-bradley/)
:::
:::

*See also:* Bruce Wydick, a development economist (Univ. of San Francisco), writes on
faith and economics at [acrosstwoworlds.net](https://www.acrosstwoworlds.net/a-christian-development-economist/)
(author of *Shrewd Samaritan*).
